metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Students explore film and television production, theory, cinematography, editing, and media literacy. This course emphasizes critical viewing, production techniques, and understanding visual storytelling across media platforms.
metadata.general_description: A course focused on film theory, production, cinematography, editing, and media literacy.

big_ideas.statement: Film and television are powerful mediums for storytelling, communication, and artistic expression.
big_ideas.elaboration: Moving image media combine visual, auditory, and narrative elements to create compelling stories and convey meaning to diverse audiences.

big_ideas.statement: Cinematography, editing, and design choices shape how audiences perceive and interpret films and shows.
big_ideas.elaboration: Technical and creative decisions such as camera angles, composition, editing pacing, and sound design directly influence audience emotion and understanding.

big_ideas.statement: Critical media literacy enables viewers to analyze, evaluate, and create media messages.
big_ideas.elaboration: Understanding how media is constructed, represented, and interpreted helps viewers think critically about the content they consume and create.

big_ideas.statement: Film and television production involves collaborative creative and technical teamwork.
big_ideas.elaboration: Making films and shows requires coordination among directors, cinematographers, editors, actors, and technical specialists working toward a unified vision.

competencies.raw: Develop film and television production skills
i_can_statements: I can operate basic video recording equipment safely and effectively
I can frame shots with compositional awareness and technical control
I can conduct pre-production planning including shot lists and storyboards
I can capture footage that meets production requirements

competencies.raw: Create short films or video projects
i_can_statements: I can conceptualize and plan short video projects
I can work collaboratively in production roles
I can capture footage appropriate for my project vision
I can produce complete short videos from concept to final output
I can edit video sequences with appropriate pacing and transitions
I can integrate audio, music, and sound design into productions
I can use editing software to refine and enhance projects
I can make technical and creative editing decisions

competencies.raw: Experiment with cinematography techniques and visual styles
i_can_statements: I can explore different camera movements and angles
I can use lighting to create mood and guide attention
I can experiment with color, composition, and visual effects
I can develop a distinctive visual style in my work

competencies.raw: Analyze film and television using film theory and critical frameworks
i_can_statements: I can identify and explain cinematographic and editing techniques
I can analyze how visual and audio elements contribute to meaning
I can apply film theory concepts to media analysis
I can recognize directorial style and authorship in films

competencies.raw: Evaluate representation, narrative, and themes in media
i_can_statements: I can identify narrative structures and storytelling techniques
I can analyze representation of diverse groups and perspectives
I can examine themes and messages in films and shows
I can recognize bias and stereotypes in media content

competencies.raw: Develop critical media literacy and evaluate media messages
i_can_statements: I can identify how media is constructed and mediated
I can recognize persuasive techniques and marketing strategies
I can evaluate the credibility and bias of media sources
I can think critically about media consumption and influence

competencies.raw: Reflect on personal film and television production practices
i_can_statements: I can evaluate the effectiveness of my creative and technical choices
I can identify areas for growth and improvement in my work
I can analyze how my productions communicate meaning to audiences
I can set goals for developing as a filmmaker

competencies.raw: Create production documentation and planning materials
i_can_statements: I can write scripts and shooting scripts
I can create storyboards and shot lists
I can develop production schedules and plans
I can organize and label footage and assets

competencies.raw: Communicate film and video concepts to collaborators and audiences
i_can_statements: I can pitch film ideas compellingly to others
I can explain my directorial and creative vision
I can give constructive feedback on productions
I can present finished work to audiences

competencies.raw: Document and present film and television analysis
i_can_statements: I can write film reviews and critical analysis
I can create presentations about films and media concepts
I can explain cinematographic and editorial techniques in words
I can share media literacy insights with others

competencies.raw: Communicate through visual media and storytelling
i_can_statements: I can tell stories effectively using moving image media
I can convey emotion and meaning through filmmaking
I can engage audiences through compelling visuals and narrative
I can use film as a vehicle for personal expression and communication

competencies.raw: Connect film and television to other art forms and disciplines
i_can_statements: I can understand film and television as combinations of visual art, music, and theatre
I can explore how narrative forms translate across media
I can integrate music, sound design, and visual art into productions
I can recognize relationships between film and literature

competencies.raw: Explore diverse films and television from global and cultural perspectives
i_can_statements: I can watch and analyze films from diverse cultures and regions
I can understand how cultural contexts influence film and television
I can appreciate non-English language films and international cinema
I can study diverse filmmakers and their contributions

competencies.raw: Engage with film and television as social and cultural commentary
i_can_statements: I can create work that addresses social or cultural themes
I can analyze how films and shows reflect historical and contemporary contexts
I can understand television and film as platforms for diverse voices
I can use media to explore meaningful questions and perspectives

competencies.raw: Build awareness of film and television careers and professional practices
i_can_statements: I can understand different roles and careers in film and television production
I can explore pathways to professional work in media production
I can study professional standards and industry practices
I can develop a portfolio of work demonstrating my skills and vision

areas.name: Cinematography and Visual Composition
topics: Depth of field and focus techniques

areas.name: Editing and Post-Production
topics: Sound design, music, and audio mixing

areas.name: Film Theory and Critical Analysis
topics: Narrative structures in film and television

areas.name: Production Process and Planning
topics: Collaboration and roles in production teams
